parentQuestion: Triglycerides are a poor source of stored energy.

options: Fats made of glycerol and three fatty acid chains
scaffolds.explain: TRIGLYCERIDES are FATS made of GLYCEROL and THREE FATTY ACID chains. They're the main form of fat stored in adipose tissue.

wrongExplains.0: Proteins are made of amino acids; triglycerides are fats.
wrongExplains.2: Carbohydrates are sugars; triglycerides are lipids.
wrongExplains.3: Vitamins are micronutrients; triglycerides are energy storage fats.

scaffolds.q: What is another common name for triglycerides?
scaffolds.explain: Triglycerides are commonly called FATS. They consist of three fatty acid chains attached to a glycerol backbone.

wrongExplains.0: Fats and proteins are different macronutrients.
wrongExplains.2: Sugars are carbohydrates; triglycerides are fats.
wrongExplains.3: Vitamins are micronutrients; triglycerides are fats.

scaffolds.q: How many calories per gram do carbohydrates provide?
scaffolds.explain: Carbohydrates provide 4 KCAL/G. This is the same as proteins (4 kcal/g), but less than fats (9 kcal/g).

scaffolds.q: How many calories per gram do fats provide?
scaffolds.explain: Fats provide 9 KCAL/G — more than TWICE the energy of carbohydrates or proteins (both 4 kcal/g).

wrongExplains.0: Carbohydrates and proteins provide 4 kcal/g; fats provide 9.
wrongExplains.2: Fats provide much more than 2 kcal/g.

scaffolds.q: Why are triglycerides efficient for energy storage?
options: They pack more than twice the energy per gram compared to carbs
scaffolds.explain: Triglycerides are efficient because they pack MORE THAN TWICE THE ENERGY per gram (9 vs 4 kcal/g) compared to carbohydrates.

wrongExplains.0: Energy density, not weight, makes fats efficient storage.
wrongExplains.2: Fats are hydrophobic (don't dissolve in water).
wrongExplains.3: Fats take longer to digest than carbs.

scaffolds.q: Where are triglycerides primarily stored in the body?
scaffolds.explain: Triglycerides are primarily stored in ADIPOSE TISSUE (fat cells). This is the body's main energy warehouse.

wrongExplains.0: Some fat is in muscles, but adipose tissue is the main storage.
wrongExplains.3: The brain uses fat but adipose tissue stores most.

scaffolds.q: Why does the body store excess energy as fat rather than glycogen?
options: Fat is more energy-dense, storing more calories in less space
scaffolds.explain: Fat is MORE ENERGY-DENSE, storing more calories in less space. At 9 kcal/g vs 4 kcal/g, fat is much more compact.

wrongExplains.0: Energy density, not ease of synthesis, is the main reason.
wrongExplains.2: The body makes glycogen but stores limited amounts.
wrongExplains.3: Biological efficiency, not taste, drives storage choice.

scaffolds.q: Are triglycerides a good or poor source of stored energy?
options: Excellent — they provide 9 kcal/g, the highest of any macronutrient
Poor — they can't be used for energy
scaffolds.explain: Triglycerides are an EXCELLENT source of stored energy. At 9 kcal/g, they provide the highest energy density of any macronutrient.

wrongExplains.0: Fats provide the most calories per gram (9 kcal/g).
wrongExplains.2: Fats are definitely used for energy, especially during fasting.
wrongExplains.3: Fats are definitely an excellent energy source.

scaffolds.q: Is this TRUE: 'Triglycerides are a poor source of stored energy'?
options: False — triglycerides are an excellent energy source at 9 kcal/g
scaffolds.explain: FALSE — Triglycerides are an EXCELLENT source of stored energy. At 9 kcal/g, they're the most energy-dense macronutrient.

wrongExplains.0: Triglycerides provide MORE energy per gram than carbs or protein.
wrongExplains.2: All three macronutrients (carbs, fats, proteins) provide energy.
